MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BYRON T. BALL, Esq. of the Ball Law Firm (Tel: 310-980-8039) for plaintiff/cross-defendant Leiasa Neckham;
Atty. GREGORY J. MORROW, Esq. of Morrow Law Group (Tel: 310-721-3833) for cross-complainant/cross-defendant Common Ground Urban Development, LLC;

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER FOLEY, DOUGLAS ROSS, and 1850 BRYANT LAND LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (CHRISTOPHER FOLEY, DOUGLAS ROSS, and 1850 BRYANT LAND LLC) motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety.

As an initial matter, the court notes that the complaint does not presently plead causes of action three [intentional misrepresentation] and four [negligent misrepresentation] against movants. The complaint provides that these claims are solely against "Defendant Kaslofsky and Does 1-50." (Complaint, 9:23, 11:22.) Plaintiff's allegations that she executed the Development Services Agreement ("DSA") do not remedy the defect. (Complaint, 10:23; 11:25.) Indeed, paragraph 16 of the complaint alleges that Common Ground Urban Development, LLC is the party that actually executed the DSA and plaintiff does not sufficiently allege facts regarding how she was an intended third party beneficiary of that agreement. "Whether a third party is an intended beneficiary or merely an incidental beneficiary to the contract involves construction of the parties' intent, gleaned from reading the contract as a whole in light of the circumstances under which it was entered." (Jones v. Aetna Casualty & Surety Co. (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 1717, 1725.) Plaintiff must calendar a motion to amend to add these defendants to causes of action three and four.

The court rejects defendants' argument that plaintiff lacks standing to maintain the interference causes of action (claims five and six). The complaint plainly alleges that defendants interfered with the Common Ground Operating Agreement and plaintiff was individually a party to that agreement. (Complaint, 7:9-10; 13:1-6.) The gravamen of these claims is that defendants interfered with the Operating Agreement and defendants' argument that plaintiff is suing to vindicate rights under the DSA is a strawman. Defendants' waiver/release argument similarly fails. Defendant adduces a Termination Agreement that Common Ground entered into with 1850 Bryant Land LLC. Those two entities agreed to release claims "arising from or in any way connected to the leasing of the Premises, or Owner's management or operation of the Building" Defendant does not cogently explain how these entities' release applies to plaintiff's individual claims. Plaintiff never signed the Termination Agreement.

Defendants fail to show that plaintiff's interference claims, which are based on the Operating Agreement, substantively fail. Defendants' separate statement of undisputed facts does not sufficiently address these claims nor does it comply with CRC 3.1350(b) [separate statement must repeat verbatim the issue to be summarily adjudicated stated in the notice of motion]. For example, defendants argue that plaintiff never signed or finalized the Operating Agreement, but no such facts are cited in the separate statement. Under the "Golden Rule" of summary judgment, evidence must generally be cited in the separate statement of undisputed facts. (See Allen v. Smith (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1282.) In any event, plaintiff presents evidence that she did in fact execute the Operating Agreement. (Beckham Depo., 189:18-190:5.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MOTION Defendants 1850 Bryant Land Llc, Christopher Paul Foley And Douglas Ross Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Constantine Panagotacos, Esq. of DUNN & PANAGOTACOS LLP, T:415.982.2100, for defendants/moving party;
Byron Ball, Esq. of the BALL LAW FIRM LLP, T:310.446.6148, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER FOLEY, DOUGLAS ROSS, 1850 BRYANT LAND LLC'S Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7, as follows:

Defendants 1850 Bryant Land LLC, Christopher Paul Foley, and Douglass Ross' motion for sanctions against Plaintiff Leiasa Beckam pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 is denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Defendants fail to carry their burden to show that Plaintiff filed the complaint primarily for an improper purpose, or that it is legally or factually frivolous. (Code Civ. Proc., 128.7(b); see San Diegans for Open Government v. City of San Diego (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 1306, 1320 ["the ultimate burden of proving sanctionable conduct remains with the moving party"], superseded by statute on other grounds as noted in In re Marriage of Sahafzadeh-Taeb and Taeb (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 124, 128.) Defendants' motion is unsupported by competent evidence sufficient to meet their burden. (Cf. Peake v. Underwood (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 428, 432, 439-440 [trial court properly awarded sanctions after finding moving defendant "met his burden to show [plaintiff's] claims were 'without legal or evidentiary support' and [plaintiff's] continued maintenance of the lawsuit demonstrated 'objective bad faith' warranting" sanctions].) That Defendants' transactional counsel sent letters to Plaintiff's counsel "explaining why [in Defendants' view] the allegations in the Complaint are false and not supported by facts" (Panagotacos Decl. 2) does not establish that conclusion, but only that Defendants so contend.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org
